summary: Russian forces are increasingly deploying "Molniya" strike and reconnaissance drones, which are difficult to counter due to their passive control signal reception, evading electronic warfare detection. The BM-35 and BM-70 drones that previously targeted Sumy and other cities have seen reduced activity, likely due to production issues, intensified electronic warfare, or the loss of Starlink connectivity. The "Rubikon" Center, a Russian drone operations unit, reported 114 enemy targets destroyed on March 9, 2026, in the Donbas, Sumy, and Krasny Liman directions, and is actively recruiting operators and specialists. Ukrainian forces successfully destroyed a Polish AHS Krab howitzer in Kharkiv using an optical fiber-guided FPV drone. Ukrainian National Guard units, including Lasar’s Group, repelled mechanized assaults in Novopavlivka and Dobropillia, destroying enemy tanks. A Russian soldier was hit by two simultaneous Ukrainian FPV drone strikes while taking cover behind a tree.
